metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the model hit the token cap before finishing (finish_reason=length) — its output is TRUNCATED; raise max_tokens or split the step

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required `gerrit_to_platform.ini` sections and keys (e.g., `[github] token_path=`, `repo=`, `workflow_id=`, `ref=`; `[gerrit] host=`, `port=`) and the on-disk path where S4's SSM materialization places the PAT.
findings.finding: The required fields and schema for `gerrit_to_platform.ini` are not specified — an agent cannot write the file without knowing which keys are mandatory (e.g., `[github]` token path, `repo`, `workflow_id`, `ref`, `[gerrit]` host/port).
findings.location: AC3: 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a representative ini template to the plan, e.g.:
```
[github]
token_path = /run/secrets/github-pat   # path written by S4 SSM hook
repo       = <org>/<repo>
workflow   = ci.yml
ref        = main

[gerrit]
host = localhost
port = 29418
```
Reference the S4 output path explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact config section, key, and value for the `recheck → verify` comment-to-vote mapping (e.g., the g2p ini `[comment_triggers]` section, trigger string regex, and label name).
findings.finding: The `recheck → verify` comment mapping is named but the configuration syntax, section, and trigger-string are not specified, leaving the executor to reverse-engineer g2p's config format.
findings.location: AC3: '+ the recheck → verify mapping'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each of ACs 1–3 and 5 (e.g., `gerrit plugin ls | grep hooks`, `ls -la $site/hooks/patchset-created`, g2p config-validate invocation, and ADR file path assertion).
findings.finding: ACs 1, 2, 3, and 5 have no associated proving commands; an executor cannot deterministically verify these criteria are satisfied without guessing at the right check.
findings.location: AC1 (plugin enable), AC2 (symlinks), AC3 (config files), AC5 (ADR)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'observe' in AC4 with a concrete proving command, e.g.: `ssh -p 29418 admin@localhost gerrit query --format=JSON change:<id> | jq .labels` to confirm the LLM-Review vote, AND `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit=1` (or equivalent API call) to confirm the workflow_dispatch was received by GitHub Actions.
findings.finding: AC4's coexistence proof uses 'observe' without specifying the concrete artifact or log line that constitutes evidence, making it a hedge rather than a verifiable proving command.
findings.location: AC4: 'push a test patchset and observe BOTH the review-bot webhook LLM-Review vote AND the g2p hook workflow_dispatch fire on the same event'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the `replication.config` symlink replaces a file written in S2 and, if so, document an explicit expand-contract sequence or rollback path (e.g., back up the original before symlinking).
findings.finding: The plan describes a 'replication.config symlink' in AC3/Scope, but S2 (a declared dependency) likely already wrote a replication.config file. If so, creating a symlink at that path is a destructive overwrite of S2's artifact, and no expand-contract sequence or rollback path is described.
findings.location: Scope / AC3 — 'replication.config symlink'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note: 'S2 writes replication.config; S3 converts it to a symlink pointing to a host-managed copy. Back up the original before conversion. Rollback: restore the backup and remove the symlink.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out step for plugin enablement (e.g., 'if Gerrit fails to restart after init --install-plugin, remove the plugin JARs from $site/plugins/ and restart') or acknowledge the restart window is acceptable and bounded.
findings.finding: Enabling the 'hooks' and 'replication' core plugins via `init --install-plugin` on a live Gerrit instance typically requires a Gerrit server restart (a service interruption). The plan names no rollback or back-out path if plugin enablement destabilizes the server.
findings.location: AC1 — 'Enable the hooks + replication core plugins (from gerrit.war via init --install-plugin / copy)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief described work item in What/Scope for the coexistence validation procedure (e.g., target repo, how to confirm workflow_dispatch fired — GitHub Actions run URL or Gerrit comment), so AC4 maps to concrete described work.
findings.finding: AC4 (coexistence proof: push a test patchset and observe both LLM-Review vote and workflow_dispatch) has no corresponding described work in the plan body — neither What nor Scope mentions a test patchset setup, a target test repository, or how the coexistence validation will be executed, leaving the criterion unmappable to any described deliverable.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Coexistence proof: push a test patchset and observe BOTH…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (and `replication.config` symlink target) are committed to `infra/gerrit/` and rendered/deployed via IaC (e.g., Ansible template or Terraform file provisioner), so the `recheck → verify` mapping and other evolving settings are version-controlled and reproducible.
findings.finding: `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (which carries the `recheck → verify` business rule and GitHub PAT path) is described as a host-side file but the plan does not say it will be version-controlled or managed via IaC/Ansible, making it an untracked, manually-edited artifact that will drift or be lost when the host is reprovisioned.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #3 / What section: 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or sub-task: 'Commit `gerrit_to_platform.ini.j2` (or `.tpl`) to `infra/gerrit/config/` and deploy it via the existing IaC provisioner; document the file's location and update procedure in the ADR or a companion runbook section.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify timeout for hook subprocess execution (e.g., Gerrit `hooks.syncHookTimeout`) and g2p's own retry/backoff policy for `workflow_dispatch` calls, plus what Gerrit does (continues vs. votes −1) when the hook exits non-zero.
findings.finding: The plan adds an external integration (g2p → GitHub Actions `workflow_dispatch` over HTTPS) with no mention of retry/backoff, timeout, or circuit-breaker, and no specification of how Gerrit behaves when the hook subprocess hangs or errors out.
findings.location: AC2 / AC3 — hook install and configuration; 'What' section describing workflow_dispatch
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: set `hooks.syncHookTimeout = 10s` (or configure async hooks) in `gerrit.config`; confirm g2p's built-in retry count and backoff; document that a non-zero hook exit is logged but does not block the patchset-created vote.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: specify where g2p hook stdout/stderr is captured (e.g., journald/syslog on the host), define a log-based alert or metric for non-zero hook exit codes, and include this in the ADR.
findings.finding: No observability (logs, metrics, or alerts) is specified for hook execution failures or missed `workflow_dispatch` calls, so operators have no signal when CI triggers are silently dropped.
findings.location: AC3 / AC4 — configuration and coexistence proof

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the RETIRE/update path for hook symlinks: specify that symlinks are re-created (idempotently) on every g2p install/upgrade via an idempotent Ansible task or install script, and that uninstall removes them.
findings.finding: The plan does not describe a RETIRE/update path for hook symlinks: if g2p is upgraded and console-script paths change, or if g2p is removed, stale symlinks in `$site/hooks/` will cause silent failures or dangling references.
findings.location: AC2 — 'symlink the patchset-created / comment-added / change-merged console-script hooks into the bind-mounted $site/hooks/'
findings.suggested_fix: Use an Ansible `file` task with `state: link` (idempotent) for creation and a corresponding `state: absent` task in a removal playbook; run the creation task as part of any g2p upgrade.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the PAT rotation lifecycle: specify how often SSM materialization re-runs, whether the credential file is read per invocation (so rotation is seamless) or cached, and what happens to in-flight hooks during a file rewrite (atomic write via temp-file rename).
findings.finding: The PAT referenced in `gerrit_to_platform.ini` is materialized from SSM by a host-side process (S4), but the plan does not specify how g2p picks up a rotated/updated PAT — whether it re-reads the file on each hook invocation or caches it — creating a stale-credential window.
findings.location: AC3 — 'gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: Ensure the SSM materialization writes via atomic rename (write to `.tmp`, then `mv`); confirm g2p reads the ini file fresh on each hook invocation rather than caching at process start.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a runtime observable to AC1 (e.g., `gerrit ls-plugins` output showing `hooks` and `replication` as active, or Gerrit admin plugin-list UI screenshot) so plugin activation is independently verifiable, not just documented.
findings.finding: AC1's observable proof of plugin enablement is only a README update ('list them in infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md'), not a runtime verification that the plugins are actually active in Gerrit. A documentation artifact is not a faithful end-state-observable proof of successful plugin activation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: 'Enable the hooks + replication core plugins … verify they appear as active via `ssh -p 29418 admin@localhost gerrit ls-plugins` (or equivalent), and list them in infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that posts a `recheck` comment on the test patchset and verifies a second `workflow_dispatch` fires (exercising the `comment-added` hook and the recheck→verify mapping end-to-end).
findings.finding: The `comment-added` / `recheck → verify` mapping is a new user-facing flow explicitly scoped in the plan, but no acceptance criterion tests it (happy path or otherwise). AC4 only validates patchset-created; no AC asks 'post a recheck comment and observe a new workflow_dispatch'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 / AC3 (recheck→verify mapping)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'Post a comment containing "recheck" on the test patchset from AC4 and confirm a new workflow_dispatch event appears in the GitHub Actions run history for that SHA.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one failure-path AC: verify that when the PAT is absent or the dispatch call fails, g2p exits with a logged error but Gerrit still accepts the patchset (hook failure is non-blocking).
findings.finding: AC4 covers only the happy-path for the patchset-created → workflow_dispatch flow; there is no failure-path criterion (e.g., PAT absent/expired, GitHub API returns non-200, hook exits non-zero) to verify that Gerrit's own patchset processing is not blocked when g2p fails.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'Temporarily remove/corrupt the PAT and push a patchset; confirm Gerrit accepts it, the hook logs an error, and no vote is withheld — proving the hook is non-blocking on failure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the g2p GitHub PAT SSM slot is added to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list and materialized by `user_data.sh` (following the existing `PARAMS` map pattern) rather than via a separate S4 loading mechanism, so all host-side secrets remain in one auditable surface.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `gerrit_to_platform.ini` as a new host-side config file that holds the GitHub PAT (a secret), creating a separate config surface and loading path rather than extending the established SSM → user_data.sh pipeline. The `ssm.tf` `rebar_secret_params` list has no slot for the g2p PAT, and `user_data.sh`'s `PARAMS` map does not include it. AC3 says 'PAT sourced via host-side SSM materialization from S4' but the delivery vehicle (a new ini file + S4-defined loading mechanism) diverges from the project-wide pattern where all host-side secrets are declared in `ssm.tf`, fetched by `user_data.sh`, and written atomically to `/etc/rebar/.env`. A second secret-materialization path makes the secret inventory split across two surfaces.
findings.location: AC3 — 'Write `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `/rebar/prod/g2p-github-pat` (or similar) to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list and the `user_data.sh` `PARAMS` map. Have the S3 setup script read the PAT from `/etc/rebar/.env` (already 0600, already managed by the fail-fast boot path) when writing `gerrit_to_platform.ini`, matching the pattern used for `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` and other host-side secrets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is present in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image (e.g., `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'ls /var/gerrit/plugins/'`) and confirm it can be enabled without an external download before writing the AC1 install procedure.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is a 'core plugin bundled in gerrit.war' that can be enabled via `init --install-plugin` / copy with no external download. No file in the codebase (`install-plugins.sh`, `plugins/README.md`, `compose-up.sh`, `docker-compose.yml`) mentions a `hooks` plugin — only `webhooks`, `replication`, `events-log`, and `oauth` appear. The hooks plugin's presence in the seeded image plugins dir is unverified.
findings.location: AC1 / What section / Scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the missing `gerrit_hooks` bind-mount (host `/var/gerrit/site/hooks` → container `/var/gerrit/hooks`) to `docker-compose.yml` and the corresponding `compose-up.sh` seed step as an explicit deliverable of S3, since it does not currently exist.
findings.finding: The plan asserts g2p hooks will be symlinked into 'the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`', treating a `/var/gerrit/hooks` bind-mount as pre-existing. The `docker-compose.yml` defines exactly 7 bind-mounted volumes (git, index, cache, db, etc, logs, plugins) — there is no `gerrit_hooks` volume and no `/var/gerrit/hooks` mount. Without this mount, symlinks placed on the host at `/var/gerrit/site/hooks/` would not be visible inside the Gerrit container where the hooks plugin invokes them.
findings.location: AC2 / What section / docker-compose.yml
findings.suggested_fix: Add to docker-compose.yml volumes: `gerrit_hooks: external: true`; add `hooks` to the `for d in ...` loop in compose-up.sh; add the volume create call; add `- gerrit_hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks` under gerrit service volumes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `dnf install -y python3.11` (or equivalent) step to `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` or a new `infra/gerrit/install-g2p.sh` script, and verify the AL2023 package name for Python 3.11 before writing the AC2 install procedure.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Install g2p on the host (Python ≥3.11)' as a simple prerequisite, but the host provisioning scripts (`user_data.sh`, `compose-up.sh`) install only `docker`, `docker-compose-plugin`, `git`, and `nvme-cli`. There is no Python 3.11+ install step anywhere in `infra/`. AL2023 ships Python 3.9 by default; Python 3.11 must be explicitly installed (e.g., `dnf install python3.11`).
findings.location: AC2 / What section
findings.suggested_fix: On AL2023, `dnf install -y python3.11` installs Python 3.11; `python3.11 -m pip install gerrit-to-platform` or creating a venv with `python3.11 -m venv /opt/g2p` then `pip install gerrit-to-platform` within it is the correct pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in S3 (or S4) how the host-side PAT materialization works mechanically — name the target file path, the script that writes it, and how `gerrit_to_platform.ini` references it without storing plaintext (e.g., a systemd EnvironmentFile, a 0600 host file at `/etc/g2p/secrets.env`, or an env-var injection at hook invocation time).
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 states 'write `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4)' and treats this host-side SSM path as a defined mechanism. Reading `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` confirms it writes only `infra/compose/.env` for container consumption — there is no host-side secret materialization path for processes like g2p that run outside Docker. The plan correctly says S4 will add it, but the S3 AC is written as if the path pre-exists.
findings.location: AC3 / Scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what the 'replication.config symlink' in AC3 refers to — if it means symlinking `infra/gerrit/replication.config` into the g2p config directory (not the Gerrit site `etc/`), state the source and target explicitly to avoid conflicting with `setup-replication.sh`'s copy to `$GERRIT_SITE/etc/replication.config`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 specifies a '`replication.config` symlink' as a deliverable, but `infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh` already installs `replication.config` as a real copied file at `$GERRIT_SITE/etc/replication.config` (line 90: `echo 'setup-replication: installing ${DST_CONFIG}'`). Creating a symlink at that path would conflict with the existing copy mechanism and could break the `setup-replication.sh` idempotency contract.
findings.location: AC3 / Scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` and/or `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` as edit targets and specify the exact mechanism (e.g., `gerrit init --install-plugin=hooks` inside a one-shot container run, or copying from the baked image) that enables the `hooks` core plugin on the live site.
findings.finding: `install-plugins.sh` and `compose-up.sh` are not enumerated as files requiring changes to enable the `hooks` core plugin, yet the existing scripts contain no logic for it.
findings.location: AC1 — 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins … list them in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (and `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh`) to the edit-set, specify whether `$site/hooks/` is a new bind-mount into the container or whether g2p hooks execute on the host via a different dispatch mechanism, and document how the Gerrit hooks plugin calls them.
findings.finding: `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` is not enumerated as an edit target, yet g2p hook symlinks in `$site/hooks/` are only visible to Gerrit's hooks plugin if that directory is bind-mounted into the container.
findings.location: AC2 — 'symlink the `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` console-script hooks into the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` and `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` (and/or a new host-side materialization script) to the edit-set, and specify the exact SSM parameter name for the g2p GitHub PAT so the secret-store pattern is closed end-to-end.
findings.finding: The SSM parameter slot for the GitHub PAT (needed by `gerrit_to_platform.ini`) is absent from `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` and `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`, yet neither file is named as an edit target.
findings.location: AC3 — 'GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `/rebar/prod/github-actions-pat` to the `rebar_secret_params` list in `ssm.tf` (with `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }`) and add a corresponding `get_param` call in `fetch-secrets.sh` (or a dedicated g2p materialization script analogous to `materialize-deploy-key.sh`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete artifact that proves AC4 coexistence (e.g., an extension to `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` that also asserts a `workflow_dispatch` event was triggered, or a new script), and add it to the edit-set.
findings.finding: The coexistence proof (AC4) requires both the review-bot webhook and g2p `workflow_dispatch` to fire on the same event, but `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` (the existing end-to-end test script) is not enumerated as an edit target despite being the natural home for this assertion.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Coexistence proof: push a test patchset and observe BOTH the review-bot webhook `LLM-Review` vote AND the g2p hook `workflow_dispatch` fire on the same event'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the `hooks` plugin (running inside the Java-only Gerrit container) invokes g2p (a host-side Python tool): either (a) add a `hooks/` bind-mount to `docker-compose.yml` AND provide a hook shim that executes via the host (e.g. a wrapper script the container can exec that delegates via a socket/volume to the host Python process), or (b) adopt an alternative dispatch mechanism such as g2p running as a host daemon reading `gerrit stream-events` over SSH rather than relying on the hooks plugin exec path.
findings.finding: The Gerrit `hooks` plugin executes hook files in the container's process space (the container is Java-only; no Python), but the plan installs g2p on the HOST and symlinks its console-script hooks into `$site/hooks/`. Two compounding gaps make this unworkable: (1) `docker-compose.yml` has no `gerrit_hooks` volume or bind-mount for the `hooks/` subdir — only `git`, `index`, `cache`, `db`, `etc`, `logs`, `plugins` are mounted — so the hooks directory isn't even shared with the host yet; (2) even if the hooks dir were bind-mounted, the symlink targets would resolve to host-side Python binaries that are not accessible from within the container (no Python in the gerrit image). The hook exec would fail with a 'not found' or 'exec format error' on every event.
findings.location: AC2: 'Install g2p on the host (Python ≥3.11) and symlink the patchset-created / comment-added / change-merged console-script hooks into the bind-mounted $site/hooks/'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC3 that 'replication.config symlink' means applying the config via the existing copy mechanism (`setup-replication.sh`), or explain the new mechanism if a symlink is genuinely intended (and specify how the repo path becomes accessible inside the container).
findings.finding: The plan says 'the `replication.config` symlink' for applying the replication config, but the existing `setup-replication.sh` script already COPYs `infra/gerrit/replication.config` into the site `etc/` directory (not a symlink). Using a symlink from the bind-mounted `etc/` volume back to a repo file would require the repo to be mounted into the container at a predictable path, which the current compose setup does not do. The mechanism description ('symlink') is inconsistent with the existing copy-based apply pattern and with how the site bind-mounts are structured.
findings.location: AC3: 'the replication.config symlink'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the replication.config symlink' with 'apply replication.config via setup-replication.sh (cp into site etc/)' to match the existing operational pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing the install approach, confirm the `hooks` plugin is bundled in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` by running `docker run --rm gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 ls /var/gerrit/plugins/` or inspecting the war's `WEB-INF/plugins/` directory, and document the confirmed init flag / copy path in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` alongside the other bundled-plugin notes.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is a core plugin bundled in `gerrit.war` and enableable via `init --install-plugin` or copy — with no download or sha256 pin needed. However, `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` (the repo's authoritative plugin provenance record) lists only `webhooks`, `events-log`, and `oauth`; the `hooks` plugin is entirely absent. `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` has no mention of it. No other file in the codebase documents the `hooks` plugin's presence or prior enablement in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image. The epic's decision log marks this 'Verified fact' but provides no in-repo evidence. The story plan inherits this unverified assertion — if `hooks` is NOT bundled in the 3.14.1 war (or has a different copy-from-war path than assumed), the entire g2p installation fails silently at hook dispatch time.
findings.location: AC1: 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins (from `gerrit.war` via `init --install-plugin` / copy — no download or sha256 pin)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a verification step to the story: `docker run --rm gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 find /var/gerrit -name 'hooks*.jar' -o -name 'hooks' 2>/dev/null` to confirm presence and path, then update `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` with a row for `hooks` (similar to the `webhooks` row) and the confirmed enablement command before writing the install script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact runtime mechanism by which g2p reads the GitHub PAT without it being written into gerrit_to_platform.ini as plaintext (e.g., a wrapper shim that fetches from SSM and injects via environment variable, or a tmpfs-backed secrets dir that is NOT on the EBS data volume and is NOT snapshotted).
findings.finding: The plan's AC says the GitHub PAT will be sourced 'via the host-side SSM materialization — no plaintext on disk', but gerrit_to_platform.ini is a config file g2p reads at hook-invocation time, and the plan does not specify the mechanism by which the PAT reaches g2p without being written into that file in plaintext. Options (env-var wrapper script, tmpfs path, on-demand SSM fetch in a shim) are not named, leaving the no-plaintext guarantee unenforceable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 3: 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a thin hook-wrapper script (e.g., `/usr/local/bin/g2p-hook-shim`) that fetches the PAT from SSM at invocation time (`aws ssm get-parameter --with-decryption`) and exports it as the environment variable g2p reads (`GITHUB_TOKEN` or equivalent), then exec's the real g2p console script. The `gerrit_to_platform.ini` on disk omits the PAT field entirely (or uses a placeholder). This keeps the PAT in memory only, never on disk, and is consistent with the existing SSM-SecureString pattern in fetch-secrets.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the host-side g2p install (pip install, hook symlinks, ini/config files) is encoded in a reproducible, idempotent artifact (e.g., extension of infra/terraform/user_data.sh or a new infra/scripts/install-g2p.sh called from user_data) so a reprovisioned instance self-heals without manual intervention.
findings.finding: The host-side installation steps (pip install gerrit-to-platform, hook symlinks into $site/hooks/, gerrit_to_platform.ini, replication.config symlink) are described imperatively with no specification of how they are made idempotent or integrated into the existing IaC/user_data reprovisioning path, leaving them vulnerable to drift if the instance is replaced or reprovisioned.
findings.location: What / Scope sections and AC 2–3: host-side g2p install + hook symlinks + ini file

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `hooks` plugin is present in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (e.g., `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'ls /var/gerrit/plugins/'`) and confirm the exact enablement command, then document it in `plugins/README.md`.
findings.finding: The plan's stated enablement mechanism for the `hooks` core plugin — 'init --install-plugin / copy — no download or sha256 pin' — is UNVERIFIED: neither `install-plugins.sh`, `plugins/README.md`, nor `compose-up.sh` mention the `hooks` plugin by name, and the plan does not confirm that `hooks` is present among the baked plugins in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1`.
findings.location: AC1: 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins (from `gerrit.war` via `init --install-plugin` / copy — no download or sha256 pin)'
findings.suggested_fix: Run `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'ls /var/gerrit/plugins/'` to list baked plugins; if `hooks.jar` is present, the compose-up seed step already copies it — update the plan to say 'already seeded by compose-up.sh' rather than 'init --install-plugin'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a bind-mount for the hooks directory to `docker-compose.yml` (e.g., `/var/gerrit/site/hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks`) and update `compose-up.sh` to create the host-side directory alongside the other site subdirs.
findings.finding: The `$site/hooks/` directory is not bind-mounted in `docker-compose.yml`, so g2p console-script hook symlinks placed on the host under that path will be invisible to the Gerrit container — the `hooks` plugin executes scripts from within the container's `$GERRIT_SITE/hooks/` path.
findings.location: AC2: 'symlink the `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` console-script hooks into the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`'
findings.suggested_fix: In `docker-compose.yml` under `gerrit.volumes`, add `- /var/gerrit/site/hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks` (direct bind, same pattern as `/var/gerrit/site/dotssh:/var/gerrit/.ssh`). In `compose-up.sh`, add `hooks` to the `for d in git index cache db etc logs plugins` loop.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step to the S3 install runbook (and/or a new `install-g2p.sh` script) that installs Python 3.11 on AL2023 via `dnf install python3.11` before running `pip install gerrit-to-platform`.
findings.finding: The plan requires Python ≥3.11 on the AL2023 host for `pip install gerrit-to-platform`, but AL2023's default `python3` is Python 3.9 and no existing host script installs Python 3.11+ — the precondition is unmet and unstated.
findings.location: AC2: 'Install g2p on the host (Python ≥3.11)'
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend `dnf install -y python3.11` to the g2p install step and invoke g2p via `python3.11 -m pip install gerrit-to-platform` (or a dedicated `/opt/g2p` venv) to avoid colliding with the system Python 3.9.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before coding, run `pip install gerrit-to-platform && pip show -f gerrit-to-platform` (or inspect `https://pypi.org/project/gerrit-to-platform/`) to verify the exact console-script entry-point names and the required `gerrit_to_platform.ini` keys, and record them in the plan.
findings.finding: The g2p console-script hook names (`patchset-created`, `comment-added`, `change-merged`) and the config file name `gerrit_to_platform.ini` are UNVERIFIED against the actual PyPI package's declared entry points and documentation.
findings.location: AC2 / AC3: 'symlink the `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` console-script hooks' and 'Write `gerrit_to_platform.ini`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether AC3's 'replication.config symlink' means a new g2p-specific config symlink at a different path, or a replacement of the S2-deployed file — and if the latter, document the `rm`+`ln -s` sequence explicitly.
findings.finding: S2 (a declared dependency) already writes `$site/etc/replication.config` as a regular file via `setup-replication.sh`; AC3's 'replication.config symlink' cannot be created at that path without first removing the existing file — the plan does not address this conflict.
findings.location: AC3: 'the `replication.config` symlink'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 the concrete mechanism by which the GitHub PAT is kept out of the gerrit_to_platform.ini file body at rest — e.g., 'fetch-secrets.sh writes gerrit_to_platform.ini per-boot with mode 0600, substituting the PAT from SSM, analogous to the .env materialization', or 'the INI references an env var (GITHUB_TOKEN) that is sourced from the SSM-materialized .env rather than embedding the literal value'.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk' but does not specify the technical mechanism by which the PAT is kept out of gerrit_to_platform.ini at rest. The existing SSM materialization (fetch-secrets.sh → mode-0600 .env) targets docker-compose containers; gerrit_to_platform.ini is a host-side file g2p reads at hook-invocation time. Without an explicit contract (e.g., the INI is written per-boot by a materialization script with mode 0600, or the PAT is read from an env var rather than embedded in the file), an implementer may write the PAT value literally into the INI file, leaving a plaintext secret on disk on the boundary-crossing path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend fetch-secrets.sh (or a parallel script) to write gerrit_to_platform.ini per boot from the SSM-stored PAT value, with mode 0600, never committing the file. Add gerrit_to_platform.ini to .gitignore. Reference this mechanism explicitly in AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 (or the What/Scope section) that the GitHub PAT for g2p must be a fine-grained PAT scoped to the single target repository (navapbc/rebar) with only the minimum required permissions (e.g., `actions: write` for `workflow_dispatch`, `contents: read` for repo metadata), not a classic org-wide PAT.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether the GitHub PAT used by g2p is a fine-grained, single-repo-scoped token or a classic (org-wide) PAT. A classic PAT grants permissions across the entire GitHub organisation; using one for a single-repo `workflow_dispatch` integration violates least-privilege on the boundary-crossing path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'The PAT must be a fine-grained token scoped to navapbc/rebar with actions:write (to fire workflow_dispatch) and metadata:read only — a classic PAT is not acceptable.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
